reason: The statement applies because the Hebrews, represented by Hezekiah and his people, are indeed in a foreign land under the oppression of the Assyrian king. The text depicts Hezekiah seeking help from the LORD and praying for deliverance from the Assyrians, acknowledging the power of Yahweh over other gods. This aligns with the idea of Yahweh being displeased with the Hebrews for turning to other gods and not solely relying on Him.
